title: Multica Managed Agents
voiceScript: What if you could assign work to AI the way you assign it to your team? Most people treat AI like a tool. Endless back-and-forth in a chat, and every useful answer gets lost in the logs. Multica treats AI agents as real teammates. Five layers turn a local daemon into named agents, a task board, squads, and scheduled autopilots. It runs five AI runtimes, retries failed tasks twice on its own, and it is completely free. AI is not a sidekick. It is part of the team. Stop chatting with your AI and start assigning to it. Follow for more.

scenes.cta: Ready to put your agents on the team?

source: Multica - open-source managed agents platform that treats AI coding agents as first-class assignable teammates (Linear/Jira-style Kanban surface) instead of chat interfaces. Core model: Workspaces, Agents (persistent identities bound to a runtime + provider: Claude Code, Codex, GitHub Copilot CLI, Cursor Agent, Gemini), Runtimes (local daemon auto-detects installed agent CLIs). 5 layers: Runtime (daemon+config, injects CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md) -> Identity (persistent agents) -> Execution (task lifecycle: Queued/Dispatched/Running/Completed-Failed, WebSocket progress, auto-retry up to 2x) -> Delegation (Squads under a leader agent + reusable Skills bundles) -> Automation (Autopilots on cron). Early production (v0.2.x); lacks deep governance/enterprise integrations; some known bugs (importing skills with binary assets).
